free_text: Energy Dependence of Product Yields in Copper
Spallation by Protons between 3 and 30 GeV
AGS
Five 0.002-in copper foils, plus four 0.001-in
aluminum foils.
Since the targets were relatively thick, the effect of
secondaries on the monitor reaction was determined
empirically. The ratio of F18 to Na24 production in
aluminum was used to correct the observed Na24 yields
for the secondary effects. The correction amounted to
3% at 30 GeV and 2% at 3 GeV.
BIB corrections; converted to lower case.
No information on uncertainties.

Errors on cross sections at each energy
arising from uncertainties in the determination of
Na24 disintegration rate in aluminum foil, and in the
disintegration rate and chemical yield of copper
spallation product was estimated. The larger of these
error estimates at 30 and 3 GeV were combined by rms
addition with the +-7% uncertainty in the monitor
cross section ratio at the two energies.
From decay of 65Cu.
Data taken from reference, Tables I and II.
